tldr: Move from ByteBrew's free game stack to Airbridge for a more mature MMP with enterprise-grade attribution.
why_switch: More mature MMP feature set and partner integrations
Better fit beyond mobile games and for non-Unity stacks
Published subscription pricing with a free install allowance
Better suited if you outgrow ByteBrew's young roadmap
what_to_export: Raw event logs and custom event schemas
Ad revenue and IAP / monetization events
Remote config keys and A/B test definitions
export_method: Use ByteBrew's available data export options to pull raw events, installs, and attribution data into your own warehouse. Specific export channels and limits should be confirmed in ByteBrew's official documentation, as the platform is relatively new and capabilities evolve.
import_method: Integrate the Airbridge SDK in a new app build and recreate networks, partners, deep links, and SKAN conversion values in the Airbridge console. Keep ByteBrew exports in cold storage for historical context. Run dual SDKs for at least one attribution window to validate counts before fully removing ByteBrew. Verify Airbridge ingestion options and partner support in current docs.

concept_mapping.note: Reconfigure partners and postbacks from Airbridge
Airbridge focus is MMP; pick a dedicated config tool
Map ad revenue and IAP into Airbridge revenue schema
Manual recreation of saved views and segments
Recreate manually; consult docs for parity

gotchas: Remote config likely needs a separate vendor
Cost goes up: Airbridge subscription vs. ByteBrew free tier
Run both SDKs in parallel for an attribution window
Re-onboard each ad network on Airbridge side
Validate SKAN setup end-to-end before cutover
Historical ByteBrew data stays for reporting only

email.subject: Migrating mobile attribution from ByteBrew to Airbridge
email.body:
Hi team,

We're planning to migrate our MMP from ByteBrew to Airbridge as we outgrow the free game-focused stack.

Plan:
1. Export raw events, installs, and attribution data from ByteBrew into our warehouse.
2. Integrate the Airbridge SDK in the next app build and reconfigure networks, deep links, and SKAN conversion values.
3. Run both SDKs in parallel for one attribution window to reconcile.
4. Pick a separate remote config tool to replace ByteBrew's remote config.

Expected effort: 1-3 weeks. Costs will go up (Airbridge subscription) in exchange for a more mature MMP.

Feedback welcome before we start.
